description: Identify organic high-density employment clusters in San Francisco by applying DBSCAN spatial clustering to buildings with 50 or more jobs. Using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/employment_clustering.ipynb, analyze data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5 to find spatial clusters using eps=1500 and min_samples=5. For each cluster, excluding noise, compute the building count, total jobs, and centroid coordinates (mean x and y). Save the aggregated cluster data, sorted by total jobs in descending order, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/employment_clusters.csv and save a 2D scatter plot of all employment nodes to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/cluster_map.png. Ensure all cells in the notebook are executed.
